_doc: Singles teambuilding archetype model (T-101, recipes re-derived T-118/T-121 from 62 Smogon OU teams, owner-validated). Each team has ONE base archetype (a slot RECIPE on a defensive↔offensive spectrum) plus a weighted, STACKABLE gimmick layer (a defining engine). The 2C engine crystallises by STRUCTURAL FIT: it scores a team's role counts against each base's `structure` recipe and takes the best fit (above a threshold); gimmicks are detected the same way and stacked. `structure` = the owner-validated slot recipe (role min/max/weight); `role` keys are the team-features the detectors tag (see featureDefinitions). `entry` is retired — crystallisation is by recipe fit, not boolean entry conditions. Values are owner-validated (docs/research/rating-decisions.md Batch 4) — hazards are ubiquitous so they are low-weight recipe slots, never a gate.

featureDefinitions.regeneratorPivot: bulky mon with Regenerator (or a bulky pivot with recovery) that switches to reset damage
featureDefinitions.hazardSetter: sets Stealth Rock / Spikes / Toxic Spikes (ubiquitous — a common slot, not archetype-defining)
featureDefinitions.wallbreaker: high-power (often Choice) attacker that breaks walls (one offensive identity per mon)
featureDefinitions.winCondition: a genuine closer — a setup sweeper
featureDefinitions.setupSweeper: boosts its own stats then sweeps (DD / SD / CM / QD / Shift Gear); high offense
featureDefinitions.priorityUser: carries a priority move as revenge / finisher
featureDefinitions.unawareWall: Unaware (or Magic Bounce) wall that denies setup / hazards
featureDefinitions.cleric: Wish / Aromatherapy / Heal Bell support on a bulky, low-offense mon
featureDefinitions.choiceScarfRevengeKiller: Choice Scarf mon used to revenge-kill / provide speed control
featureDefinitions.pivotUser: U-turn / Volt Switch / Flip Turn / Teleport pivot
featureDefinitions.focusSashLead: frail Focus Sash suicide lead (retired from recipes; the hyper-offense lead is the hazard setter)
featureDefinitions.weatherSetter: sets weather via ability (Drizzle / Drought / Sand Stream / Snow Warning)
featureDefinitions.weatherAbuser: Swift Swim / Chlorophyll / Sand Rush / Slush Rush, or a weather-boosted attacker
featureDefinitions.screenSetter: Reflect / Light Screen / Aurora Veil on a non-attacker support
featureDefinitions.trickRoomAbuser: slow, high-offense mon that benefits from Trick Room

baseArchetypes.name: Balance
baseArchetypes.description: A Regenerator/pivot backbone + a wall soaks hits and wins the one-sided hazard game (sets its own, Defogs the foe's), feeding 1-2 wallbreakers and a setup win condition.

baseArchetypes.name: Bulky Offense
baseArchetypes.description: The broad offensive default: 2-3 wallbreakers + a Choice-Scarf revenge killer + VoltTurn pivots apply relentless pressure over a thin defensive glue, closing with a setup win condition.

baseArchetypes.name: Hyper Offense
baseArchetypes.description: All-out: a hazard lead + a stack of 2-3 speed-boosting setup sweepers race to win, backed by priority (optionally behind screens).

baseArchetypes.name: Full Stall
baseArchetypes.description: A near-passive wall stack: physical + special walls split the load, an Unaware/Magic Bounce wall denies setup/hazards, clerics + Wish sustain, and hazard control keeps it healthy.

gimmicks.name: Weather (Rain/Sun/Sand/Snow)
gimmicks.description: A weather setter (by ability) powers 1-2 abusers (Swift Swim/Chlorophyll Speed, boosted attacks), with a weather-independent backup win condition.

gimmicks.name: Screens
gimmicks.description: A screen setter blunts incoming hits so a stack of 2-3 setup sweepers boosts safely behind them (a screens hyper offense).

gimmicks.name: Trick Room
gimmicks.description: Inverts the speed order so slow, high-power abusers move first — 1-2 TR setters + 2-3 slow abusers.
